---
applyTo: '**/*.4dm'
---

# 4D AIKit Component Guidelines

Documentation: <https://developer.4d.com/docs/aikit/overview>

**ALWAYS use 4D AI Kit for AI-related operations** — never use raw `4D.HTTPRequest` to call AI provider APIs.

## Client Creation

```4d
// OpenAI
var $client : Object:=cs.AIKit.OpenAI.new("your-api-key")

// Compatible providers (Ollama, LM Studio, etc.) — apiKey is required even for local providers
var $client : Object:=cs.AIKit.OpenAI.new({apiKey: "lm-studio"; baseURL: "http://localhost:11434/v1"})  // Ollama
var $client : Object:=cs.AIKit.OpenAI.new({apiKey: "lm-studio"; baseURL: "http://localhost:1234/v1"})   // LM Studio
```

> **Gotcha:** `cs.AIKit.OpenAI.new({baseURL: ...})` without `apiKey` returns Null. Always pass a dummy `apiKey` (e.g. `"lm-studio"`) for local providers that don't require authentication.

### Compatible Provider Base URLs

| Provider | baseURL |
|----------|---------|
| Ollama | `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1` |
| LM Studio | `http://localhost:1234/v1` |
| Mistral | `https://api.mistral.ai/v1` |
| DeepSeek | `https://api.deepseek.com` |
| Anthropic | `https://api.anthropic.com/v1` |
| Groq | `https://api.groq.com/openai/v1` |

## Models — List Available

```4d
var $result : Object:=$client.models.list()
If ($result.success)
    var $m : Object
    For each ($m; $result.models)
        // $m.id is the model identifier
    End for each
End if
```

> **Gotcha:** The property is `$client.models` (plural), not `$client.model`. Using `$client.model` returns Undefined.

## Chat Completion

```4d
var $messages : Collection:=[{role: "user"; content: $prompt}]
var $result : Object:=$client.chat.completions.create($messages; {model: "gpt-4o-mini"})
```

### Response Structure

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `$result.success` | Boolean | Whether the API call succeeded |
| `$result.choice` | Object | The first choice object — **NOT a Text value** |
| `$result.choice.message.content` | Text | The actual generated text |
| `$result.errors` | Collection | Error details on failure |

### Common Mistake

```4d
// ❌ WRONG — $result.choice is an Object, not Text
This.summaryText:=$result.choice

// ✅ CORRECT — extract the text content
This.summaryText:=$result.choice.message.content
```

## Embeddings

```4d
// Single text → returns 4D.Vector directly
var $result : Object:=$client.embeddings.create($text; "text-embedding-ada-002")
var $vector : 4D.Vector:=$result.vector

// Collection of texts → returns collection of 4D.Vector
var $result : Object:=$client.embeddings.create($inputs; "text-embedding-ada-002")
var $vectors : Collection:=$result.vectors
```

## Image Generation

```4d
var $result : Object:=$client.images.generate("A futuristic city"; {model: "dall-e-3"; size: "1024x1024"})
If (($result.success) && ($result.image#Null))
    $result.image.saveToDisk($file)       // save to 4D.File
    var $picture : Picture:=$result.image.asPicture()  // get as Picture
    var $blob : 4D.Blob:=$result.image.asBlob()        // get as Blob
End if
```

Use `response_format: "b64_json"` to get data inline (avoids extra download).

## Common Result Properties (OpenAIResult)

All AI Kit results inherit from `OpenAIResult`:

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `success` | Boolean | Whether HTTP request succeeded |
| `errors` | Collection | Collection of errors (network or API) |
| `terminated` | Boolean | Whether HTTP request was terminated |
| `usage` | Object | Token usage info if available |

## Async Pattern with `formula` — No CALL WORKER Needed

AIKit exposes the underlying `4D.HTTPRequest` async callbacks (see `4d.instructions.md` → "4D.HTTPRequest — Async Callbacks") through a `formula` property on parameter objects. When `formula` is set, the API call returns **immediately** and the formula is invoked when the response arrives during the form's event loop.

**This is the preferred pattern for non-blocking UI during AI operations — do NOT use CALL WORKER / CALL FORM.**

### Async Embeddings

```4d
var $options : cs.AIKit.OpenAIEmbeddingsParameters:=cs.AIKit.OpenAIEmbeddingsParameters.new()
$options.formula:=This.onEmbedDone  // reference to a form class method
$client.embeddings.create($text; $model; $options)
// returns immediately — callback fires when done
```

### Async Chat Completions

```4d
var $options : cs.AIKit.OpenAIChatCompletionsParameters:=cs.AIKit.OpenAIChatCompletionsParameters.new()
$options.model:="gpt-4o-mini"
$options.formula:=This.onChatDone
$options.stream:=True  // optional: get incremental delta.text via repeated callbacks
$client.chat.completions.create($systemPrompt; $options)
```

### Callback Signature

The callback receives a typed result object:

```4d
Function onEmbedDone($result : cs.AIKit.OpenAIEmbeddingsResult)
    If (Form#Null)
        If (($result.success) && ($result.terminated))
            // Use $result.vector
        End if
    End if
```

### Subform Callback Warning

AIKit callbacks fire in the main process event loop. `Form` in the callback refers to the **top-level DIALOG form**, NOT a subform. Always use the `$self:=This` capture pattern — see **`4d.instructions.md` → "Formula and Callbacks"**.

```4d
// ✅ In subforms — capture self
var $self : Object:=This
$options.formula:=Formula($self._onEmbedResponse($1))
```

### Recursive Chaining for Batch Processing

Process entities one-at-a-time by having the callback trigger the next call:

```4d
Function _processNext()
    If (This._pending.length=0)
        // Done
        return
    End if
    var $entity : Object:=This._pending.first()
    This._pending:=This._pending.slice(1)
    This._current:=$entity
    var $self : Object:=This
    var $options : cs.AIKit.OpenAIEmbeddingsParameters:=cs.AIKit.OpenAIEmbeddingsParameters.new()
    $options.formula:=Formula($self._onResult($1))
    $client.embeddings.create($entity.text; $model; $options)

Function _onResult($result : cs.AIKit.OpenAIEmbeddingsResult)
    If (($result.success) && ($result.terminated))
        This._current.vector:=$result.vector
        This._current.save()
        This._done+=1
    End if
    This.progress:=Int(This._done/This._total*100)
    This._processNext()  // chain to next entity
```

> **Key insight:** Each callback runs in the form's context via the event loop, so the UI refreshes naturally between each entity. No CALL WORKER or CALL FORM needed. Use `$self` capture in subforms to ensure `This` is the correct object.
